plate: 28 — 'Narcissus at the Well' (Amore sui inardescens Narcissus in florem transmutatur)
source: The Metropolitan Museum of Art, Open Access (obj. 401131)
license_note: Met flags isPublicDomain:true; Tempesta d.1630 so PD by age as well.
depicts: Narcissus gazing at his own reflection at the spring (Echo subsumed into the same scene — no separate Echo plate exists in this set).

edition: Baur (designs 1641; Nuremberg 1703 reissue) — UVM 'Ovid Project' scan
plate: 33 — 'Narcissus in Florem' (Cum sese pulcher Narcissus spectat in unda / et secum ludit, flos fit amore finis)
source: The Ovid Project, University of Vermont (Hope Greenberg)
license_note: Baur d.1642; 1703 print is PD by age. Plate no. confirmed via UVa 'Ovid Illustrated' Tempesta-Baur concordance + Wellcome record.
depicts: Narcissus sees his reflection, falls in love, and is turned into a flower (the transformation compressed into one plate).

plate: Narcissus at the spring (exists; verify on Wikimedia Commons 'Category:Virgil Solis')
source: Wikimedia Commons (URL unverified — Commons API was unreachable during research)
license_note: Solis d.1562 — PD by age. The plate is known to exist; only the direct Commons file URL is unconfirmed. FOLLOW-UP: grab the file URL via a browser.

plate: 'Narcisse epris de sa propre beaute' (Utpictura18 #2529 / Gallica BnF)
license_note: Salomon d.~1561 — PD by age. Plate exists (catalogued at Utpictura18/Gallica); direct Commons URL unconfirmed. FOLLOW-UP via browser.
depicts: Narcissus in love with his own reflection.
